purpose: Separate diagnostic futureLag metrics from deployment gates for v21 step06.

diagnostic.description: Step05 legacy rowWeighted futureLag p95 over normal-moving rows plus all train/validation rows.
All evaluation rows, including train, validation, test, robustness, static, and non-moving rows.
Train and validation all-row futureLag used to explain product's nearest-rank zero artifact.
Robustness split normal-moving futureLag. Reported for awareness but not the normal-motion product integration gate.

deploymentGates.threshold: candidate <= product + max(0.05 px, 5% of product)
candidate <= product + max(0.10 px, 5% of product)

deploymentGates.slice: split in test or robustness and StaticHold

toleranceJustification.futureLagP95: 0.05 px absolute or 5% relative is below a visible pixel and distinguishes material held-out lag regression from nearest-rank diagnostic artifacts.
toleranceJustification.futureLagP99: 0.10 px absolute or 5% relative allows a slightly wider tail tolerance while remaining subpixel.
toleranceJustification.stationaryJitterP95: 0.05 px absolute tolerance prevents product's exact zero from turning tiny numerical noise into a deployment blocker.

decisionLanguage.pass: Ready for a product-integration experiment, not automatically final product.
decisionLanguage.fail: Do not integrate; open the next POC step to calibrate failed held-out deployment slices.
